SRQ1 retrain FROM SCRATCH on the corrected pipeline — brand×month only (DEC-GRAIN), 4 categories + pooled, model set as implemented (SeasonalNaive/Ridge/LGB/XGB untuned ladder + Optuna-tuned LGB/XGB + ARIMA/Prophet), folding fixes V2 (mean-MAPE suppress), V3 (promo_intensity leakage shift/drop), V4 (market_id assert); re-verify pooled vs specialized

tasks.title: R1 finding: suppress/flag degenerate mean-MAPE in SRQ1 outputs (billions %)

R-featbuild (stage-4 review) finding: promo_intensity is target leakage — denominator is sales_units_t, i.e. the target; consumed at current-t in srq1_benchmark_tuned FEATURES. Shift(1) or drop, then re-score CSD + energidrikke (danskvand/RTD are promo-zero → unaffected)
tasks.note: CONFIRMED: build_feature_matrix.py:237 + build_feature_matrix_bychain.py:179 compute promo_units_t/sales_units_t; scripts/srq1_benchmark_tuned.py:38 uses promo_intensity as a current-t feature vs log_sales_units target (line 90). weighted_distribution (also current-t, line 38) is a softer contemporaneous-covariate concern — decide separately. Free, no API.

tasks.title: R-featbuild (stage-4 review) finding: assert exactly one market_id for DVH EXCL. HD — build_feature_matrix.py:133 guards len==0 but not >1; if Nielsen ever ships two ids under that label the 6.16x double-count silently returns. Add assert len(scope_ids)==1 to both builders
tasks.note: correctness keystone is unguarded. bychain builder filters via LEAF_CHAINS isin (multi-id by design) so lower risk there, but same class of check. Free, no API.

Nielsen warehouse reachable DIRECTLY — RU service principal + pyodbc, read-only VERIFIED (csd_clean_facts_v = 9,824,601 rows). Token minted from RU_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/TENANT via token_swap flow; same live source Prometheus uses.
tasks.note: Corrects earlier 'need Nika' assumption (that was from the April planning docs). Live source available for exploration + the code-as-action baseline. .venv has pyodbc 5.3.0; ODBC Driver 18 installed.

tasks.title: DECISION (Enrico 2026-07-12): use frozen APRIL snapshots for the reproducible SRQ4/benchmark comparison — NOT the live warehouse. Rationale: reproducible numbers + independence from RU creds/uptime.
tasks.note: Consequence 1: the code-as-action arm must also read the snapshot (not live SQL) for a fair same-data comparison → feeds the sandbox/baseline decision. Consequence 2: the CSD April snapshot is PARTIAL (a slice of the 9.8M) → see OPEN-CSD.

tasks.title: OPEN — CSD data (Enrico has NOT decided yet). STEP 1 (understand the slice, FIRST): local April CSD snapshot = 2,535,464 rows = ONLY 25.8% of the warehouse's 9,824,601 (~74% of CSD data MISSING). Understand WHAT dimension is truncated (periods? markets? products?) before deciding. STEP 2 (decide): keep partial snapshot (CSD SRQ1 on ~26% of data) vs one-time full fetch of 9.8M + freeze (complete + reproducible; fetch proven in WH1).
tasks.note: Enrico has NOT chosen either option. The decision is blocked on first understanding what the partial slice actually excludes (a 74% gap is large enough to matter for CSD result validity). NARROWED 2026-07-12 (DEC-GRAIN): the operative question is now whether brand×month DVH EXCL. HD coverage is complete in the April snapshot — the missing 74% may well be chain/region/market detail no longer in scope. Slice analysis (STEP 1) decides; Enrico wants this LAST.

tasks.title: DECISION (Enrico 2026-07-12): base LLM for BOTH SRQ4 arms = gpt-5.5 (Prometheus's production model), identical decoding settings (temp 0), per the Ch5 §5.7 same-LLM validity control.
tasks.note: Consequences: srq4_experiment.py stand-in (currently Claude) must move to gpt-5.5; re-check T04/T05/T07 cost estimates on OpenAI pricing; Ch3 §3.6 / Ch5 §5.5 same-base-LLM wording stays valid.

tasks.title: DECISION (Enrico 2026-07-12): SRQ4 = 3 arms, all gpt-5.5 temp 0 — Arm A: Prometheus + dedicated tuned-model tool (the artefact); Arm B: code-as-action (writes/runs/self-corrects its own code on the April snapshot in the sandbox = Prometheus as-is behaviour); Arm C floor: plain LLM, no tools, no code execution.
tasks.note: Arm C implemented as plain gpt-5.5 (NOT literal ChatGPT product) to preserve the same-LLM control (DEC-LLM). Closes SRQ4-protocol open decision #7. Matches Brian's 'with and without the trained models, vs a regular LLM' framing.

tasks.title: DECISION (Enrico + Brian 2026-07-12): SRQ1 scope fixed to brand×month grain ONLY — (4 categories + 1 pooled) × the model set as implemented; chain/region grains dropped from active results and documented as limitation (insufficient historical depth per region/chain) + future work, alongside the need for more extensive datasets.
tasks.note: Consequences: B02 dropped, T03 resolved (danskvand stays brand×month), V1 dropped, S01 retrain created. Pooled-vs-specialized re-verified FROM SCRATCH on the corrected pipeline, not carried over from the April results. Matches the Ch3 §3.3 locked granularity (brand aggregated across retailers, monthly).

tasks.title: OPEN (narrowed 2026-07-12): (a) real Prometheus (graph-engine) vs faithful re-implementation for arms A/B — deferred until SPIKE1 reports; (b) snapshot access in the sandbox: parquet direct (simpler) vs local SQL layer e.g. DuckDB (T-SQL fidelity).
tasks.note: Arms structure + base LLM already decided (DEC-ARMS, DEC-LLM). Enrico decides with the spike data in hand. Supersedes the srq4_experiment.py stand-in either way.

tasks.title: OPEN — SRQ4 protocol parameters (spec §8, close in one batch before the T04 smoke test): (1) K repetitions per prompt, 3 or 5 (T07 cost scales ~linearly with K); (2) numeric correctness tolerance, exact vs ±x%; (3) judge model + which qualitative dimensions to keep — NOTE: base LLM is now gpt-5.5 (DEC-LLM), so GPT-4o is NO LONGER a valid independent judge (same OpenAI family, self-evaluation bias) → pick a different family (e.g. Claude or Gemini); (4) confirm dropping the old static-template baseline (spec itself recommends drop).
tasks.note: All four are quick calls with no code prerequisite. The judge-family point also touches Ch8 §8.3.1 wording (GPT-4o named there for SRQ2 — that one stays valid because the SRQ2 synthesis model was Claude, but the SRQ4 judge must differ from gpt-5.5).
